title: IREN to Report Q3 Earnings: Should You Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
text:
IREN Limited is scheduled to announce its third-quarter fiscal 2026 results on May 7, 2026. The Zacks Consensus Estimate projects revenues of $213 million, representing 43.8% year-over-year growth, though the company is expected to report a loss of 18 cents per share compared to earnings of 11 cents in the prior year.

Key Performance Concerns: The company has missed earnings expectations in each of the last four quarters, with an average negative surprise of 205%.

Major Factors Affecting Q3 Results: Positive Drivers: Expansion of AI cloud capacity to 150,000 GPUs, including over 50,000 NVIDIA B300 units; Revenue contributions from the Microsoft contract; $2.3 billion annualized revenue run rate under contract; $9.3 billion in funding secured.

Headwinds: Declining Bitcoin mining revenues as the company transitions to AI cloud services; Sequential revenue decline of 23% in Q2, primarily from mining business; Impairment charges expected to continue, totaling $31.8 million in Q2.

Investment Recommendation: Rating HOLD due to premium valuation (P/S ratio of 6.29X versus industry average of 2.99X), intense competition from Applied Digital and TeraWulf, and execution risks in scaling the nascent AI cloud business.
